description: Legal and ethical compliance checklist for all PantryMate customer-facing content. Shield's FAIL is absolute — no item may be overridden by Godfather without Wolfgang's explicit approval. When in doubt, Shield flags and stops.
authority_note: Shield operates with maximum authority on compliance matters. A Shield FAIL is not a suggestion. Work stops until the issue is resolved or Wolfgang explicitly approves the exception in writing.

verdict_rules.pass: All items pass → content cleared for delivery/launch
verdict_rules.fail: Any item fails → escalate to Wolfgang immediately with specific violation noted. Do NOT attempt to fix and re-review without Wolfgang's knowledge.
verdict_rules.note: Unlike other reviewers, Shield failures always go to Wolfgang — not just back to the specialist.

criteria.label: TCPA: AI Disclosure in First 5 Seconds
criteria.legal_basis: Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) + FTC guidelines on AI disclosure in automated calls
criteria.description: Any AI-initiated phone call must clearly disclose that the caller is an AI, not a human, within the first 5 seconds of the call. This disclosure must be explicit — not implied.
criteria.pass_condition: Script contains an explicit AI disclosure statement in the opening section, positioned within the first 3–4 spoken lines (approx. 5 seconds at natural speaking pace).
acceptable_disclosure_phrases: I'm a virtual assistant, not a human
This is an automated AI call
I'm calling on behalf of [company] — I should mention I'm an AI
You're speaking with an AI calling on behalf of
fail_conditions: AI disclosure appears after the pitch (too late)
AI disclosure is implied but not stated ('automated system' without 'AI')
Disclosure is present only in a disclaimer footer of the script, not spoken
No AI disclosure at all
automated_checks: Find OPENING section of script
Search first 150 words for disclosure keywords: AI, artificial intelligence, virtual assistant, automated, not a human
Flag if disclosure keywords not found in first 150 words
Flag scripts where 'human' or 'person' is used to describe the caller
criteria.violation_consequence: TCPA violation risk. Immediate escalation to Wolfgang. Do not deploy.

criteria.label: DNC: Opt-Out Mechanism Present and Honored
criteria.legal_basis: Do Not Call Registry (FTC), CAN-SPAM Act, general consent principles
criteria.description: Every outreach mechanism must have a clear, functional opt-out path. Verbal opt-outs during calls must be honored immediately. Email opt-outs must work. No retry logic after opt-out.

pass_conditions.call_scripts: Script includes explicit handling for 'remove me from your list', 'don't call again', 'stop calling' — with immediate graceful exit and no re-engagement attempt.
pass_conditions.emails: Email includes working unsubscribe link or reply-to-unsubscribe instruction. Link must be functional, not # or placeholder.
pass_conditions.pages: If page collects contact info, privacy policy and opt-out terms must be linked.

fail_conditions: Call script has no opt-out handler
Call script attempts to re-engage after explicit 'remove me' request
Email has no unsubscribe mechanism
Unsubscribe link is # or broken
Page collects phone/email with no mention of how to unsubscribe
automated_checks: For call scripts: search for opt-out keywords in GRACEFUL_EXIT section: 'remove', 'opt out', 'do not call', 'stop'
For emails: search for 'unsubscribe' link or instruction
For emails: verify unsubscribe href is not '#' or placeholder
For pages with forms: check for privacy policy link near form
criteria.violation_consequence: Legal exposure. Immediate escalation to Wolfgang.

criteria.label: No Guaranteed Revenue or Income Claims
criteria.legal_basis: FTC Act Section 5 (unfair or deceptive acts), FTC Endorsement Guides
criteria.description: Content must never guarantee specific income, revenue, or financial outcomes. Results may vary disclosures are required near any earnings-related claims.
criteria.pass_condition: Any mention of financial results is framed as a possibility (not a certainty), is accompanied by context, and does not use guarantee language.
acceptable_framing: In our experience, businesses like yours may...
Results vary, but clients have reported...
This could help you increase revenue — though results depend on...
automated_checks: Case-insensitive scan for all forbidden_phrases
Scan for patterns: 'guarantee' within 5 words of dollar amount or revenue/income/profit/earn
Scan for patterns: 'will make' or 'will earn' near dollar amounts
Flag any specific dollar amount claim without 'results may vary' disclaimer nearby
criteria.violation_consequence: FTC compliance risk. Escalate to Wolfgang.

criteria.label: No Manufactured Urgency
criteria.description: Urgency tactics must only be used if the constraint is real and verifiable. 'Only 3 spots left' is only acceptable if there are actually only 3 spots left, with a mechanism to enforce it.
criteria.pass_condition: No urgency language present, OR urgency language is used for a genuinely limited offer with verifiable constraints.
forbidden_if_unverified: We can only take [N] more clients
acceptable_urgency: Pricing locked until [specific date] — this is our launch rate (if true)
We're onboarding in batches of 10 (if true and enforced)
automated_checks: Scan for urgency trigger words: 'only X spots', 'limited spots', 'expires', 'ends tonight', 'last chance', 'this week only'
Flag any countdown or timer references
Flag any claim of scarcity without verifiable mechanism
criteria.violation_consequence: Deceptive practice risk. Return to specialist for removal or verification.

criteria.label: No Impersonation of Known Brands
criteria.legal_basis: Trademark law, FTC Act, CAN-SPAM anti-deception provisions
criteria.description: Content must not imply affiliation with, endorsement by, or be from well-known third-party brands (Google, Amazon, DoorDash, Yelp, etc.) unless PantryMate has a documented partnership.
criteria.pass_condition: Content is clearly from PantryMate. No third-party brand names used in ways that imply partnership or official endorsement.
fail_conditions: Email appears to be from Google, Amazon, or another brand
Subject line implies third-party endorsement: 'Your Google Business listing...'
Call script introduces caller as representing a third-party brand
Page uses third-party logos without licensed permission
Using 'Google' or 'Yelp' branded colors/styles to imply official communication
allowed_references: Mentioning Google in context ('We help you get more Google reviews') — fine
Saying 'This is not affiliated with Google' — fine
automated_checks: Check sender/from name for third-party brand names
Check subject line for third-party brand names in potentially misleading contexts
Scan opening of call scripts for false affiliation claims
Flag use of official logos (src containing 'google-logo', 'amazon-logo', etc.)
criteria.violation_consequence: Trademark/legal risk. Escalate to Wolfgang immediately.

criteria.label: CAN-SPAM: Unsubscribe Mechanism Present
criteria.description: All commercial emails must include a clear, functional mechanism for recipients to opt out of future emails. This must be honored within 10 business days.
criteria.pass_condition: Email contains either: (a) a working unsubscribe link, OR (b) a clear reply-to instruction to unsubscribe. The mechanism must be visible (not hidden in 4pt font).
required_elements: Unsubscribe link OR clear opt-out instruction
Physical mailing address or P.O. box of sender (CAN-SPAM requirement)
From address must be identifiable as PantryMate
fail_conditions: No unsubscribe link or instruction
Unsubscribe link is # or broken
Unsubscribe text is hidden (font-size < 10px, color matches background)
No sender physical address
From: header is spoofed or misleading
automated_checks: Search for 'unsubscribe' in email body (case-insensitive)
If found, check associated href is not '#' or empty
Check for physical address in email footer
Verify From: address is @pantrymate.net domain
criteria.violation_consequence: CAN-SPAM violation. Up to $50,753 per email in fines. Escalate to Wolfgang.

criteria.label: No Misleading Subject Lines
criteria.legal_basis: CAN-SPAM Act — prohibits deceptive subject lines
criteria.description: Subject lines must not deceive recipients about the content or purpose of the email. No fake 'Re:' or 'Fwd:' prefixes on cold emails. No misleading claims in the subject.
criteria.pass_condition: Subject line accurately reflects the email content and does not use deceptive framing.
fail_conditions: Subject starts with 'Re:' when there is no prior conversation
Subject starts with 'Fwd:' when email is not a forward
Subject implies the recipient requested the email when they didn't
Subject contains a question that the email doesn't answer
Subject implies urgency or action is required when it's just an offer
Subject contains false statements about the recipient ('Your account has been...')
automated_checks: Check subject for 'Re:' prefix on cold outreach emails
Check subject for 'Fwd:' prefix
Flag subjects implying transactional urgency: 'Action required', 'Your account', 'Invoice', 'Confirmation' on clearly promotional emails
Cross-reference subject keyword themes with body keyword themes
criteria.violation_consequence: CAN-SPAM violation + trust damage. Escalate to Wolfgang.

violations: list of failed criteria with legal risk level and specific text found
output_format.reviewer_notes: Shield's assessment and mandatory next steps
output_format.action: PASS → proceed | FAIL → escalate to Wolfgang immediately
